title: Brindaban Chandra's Math
tagline: Brindaban Chandra's Math — spiritual town of West Bengal

overview.short: Brindaban Chandra's Math, or Brindaban Chandra Math, is a complex of 17th–18th century temples at Guptipara in Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal.
overview.description: Brindaban Chandra's Math, or Brindaban Chandra Math, is a complex of 17th–18th century temples at Guptipara in Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Mirdhanga
topPlaces.description: Mirdhanga is a census town in Balagarh CD Block in Chinsurah subdivision of Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Badhagachhi
topPlaces.description: Badhagachhi is a census town in Balagarh CD Block in Chinsurah subdivision of Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Guptipara
topPlaces.description: Guptipara is a village in Balagarh, a community development block that forms an administrative division in the Sadar subdivision of the Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Beharia, Nadia
topPlaces.description: Beharia is a census town in the Santipur CD block in the Ranaghat subdivision of the Nadia district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Ghoralia
topPlaces.description: Ghoralia is a census town in the Santipur CD block in the Ranaghat subdivision of the Nadia district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Shantipur
topPlaces.description: Shantipur is a city and a municipality in the Ranaghat subdivision of Nadia district in the Indian state of West Bengal. The fort area of this city, also known as Daak-Garh (calling or gathering room) is thought to have been built by Raja Krishnachandra of Nadia.

topPlaces.name: Shashpur
topPlaces.description: Shashpur is a census town in Kalna II CD Block in Kalna subdivision of Purba Bardhaman district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Santipur (community development block)
topPlaces.description: Santipur is a community development block that forms an administrative division in Ranaghat subdivision of Nadia district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Brindaban Chandra's Math?
faq.a: The best time to visit Brindaban Chandra's Math is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Brindaban Chandra's Math?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (60 km) and the nearest railway station is Howrah Junction (Kolkata) (69 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Brindaban Chandra's Math?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Brindaban Chandra's Math at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Brindaban Chandra's Math?
faq.a: Stays in Brindaban Chandra's Math start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Brindaban Chandra's Math?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mirdhanga, Badhagachhi, Guptipara, Beharia, Nadia, Ghoralia.
